manifest.name: pokemon-price
manifest.author: DreamLayer Team
manifest.description: Point at a Pokémon card and see what it's worth — condition-adjusted market price with a low–high band and an as-of date, matched against the Pokémon TCG API. Flags your grails and tallies your haul as you scan a table. Bring your own free key for heavy use.
manifest.forwho: For collectors, sorters, and anyone holding a card they can't quite value.
long: Look at a Pokémon card and the panel names the printing and tells you the going rate — the market price with a low–high band and the date it's from.
Built for the card-show table: dial in the card's condition (NM/LP/MP/HP/DMG) and the price adjusts the way a vendor would, and a running haul tally sums the singles as you scan them.
Matched live against the Pokémon TCG API (pokemontcg.io). Add your own free API key once for heavy use and it's remembered across sessions.
Only the card's printed name leaves the device to do the lookup — the card, and that it's yours, stays yours.
Concept credit: WatsonMLDev's pokemon-cost-scraper — the original Halo card-value idea and the vendor workflow this lens is built around.

source:
from dreamlayer.plugins.pokemon_price import pokemon_price_plugin


def plugin():
    # first-party WorldLens connector, bundled with the app; enabling it
    # registers the Pokémon Price object provider. Identifies a trading card
    # you look at (object_lens) and prices it against the Pokémon TCG API
    # (pokemontcg.io, urllib -> network).
    return pokemon_price_plugin()
